body_txt: Although the stigma around needing glasses has largely been gone since roughly the Turn of the Millennium , bespectacled individuals can and do still face harassment because of their bad eyesight. Insulting somebody for wearing glasses is a common tactic for bullies , especially the Alpha Bitch and Jerk Jock . "Four eyes" and "googly eyes" are classic mean nicknames for glass-wearers. "Freak," "geek," and the ever-popular "nerd" are also frequently part of the repertoire for glasses-based bullying. Some bullies may even go a step further by either stealing other people's glasses and putting them on to mock their victims , which frequently involves the bullies playing Keep Away with the glasses, or outright breaking said specs (both situations may involve bullies intentionally knocking their victim's glasses off their face ). Bullying like this may drive a glass-wearer to ditch their specs altogether despite whatever vision problems they have, which can prove problematic if they're Blind Without 'Em (and they often are). Although this can and does happen to people of all genders and sexes, bespectacled girls and women are the most common victims because of Double Standards about physical appearances. Not helping matters is the fact that Hollywood has always viewed glasses as homely , nerdy , and/or downright ugly . Sadly Truth in Television , as many bullies in Real Life target those who wear glasses, and several studies show that bespectacled children are more likely to be bullied than clear-faced kids due to being perceived as different or weak. Additionally, ripping someone’s glasses off their face can be considered a form of assault. All that said, due to the sensitive nature of this topic by virtue of being about getting bullied for something out of one's control, No Real Life Examples, Please! . Compare Braces-Based Bullying and Bullying the Disabled , the former of which may overlap with this. Related to Blind Without 'Em , Dropped Glasses , The Glasses Gotta Go , Nerd Glasses , Unwanted Glasses Plot , and You Wouldn't Hit a Guy with Glasses , all of which may overlap with this. Might lead to the more negative portrayals of Glasses Curiosity . Contrast Bespectacled Cutie and Glasses Are Sexy , which depict wearing glasses in a far more positive light.
